pattern: Treating each value as a pointer to an index turns the array into a linked structure with a cycle whose entrance is the repeated number, located by Floyd tortoise-and-hare without modifying the input.
hints: With n+1 values drawn from 1..n, the pigeonhole principle guarantees a repeat; the trick is finding it without sorting or extra space.
Read nums[i] as a next-node pointer: following the chain from index 0 must eventually revisit a node, and that revisited node is the duplicate.
Run a slow and fast pointer to find a meeting point inside the cycle, then reset one pointer to the start and advance both one step at a time until they meet again at the duplicate.

edge_inputs.description: duplicate appears twice in a small array
the same value repeated many times
duplicate is the maximum value n
duplicate is one, appearing more than twice

stress.description: permutation of 1..n with one extra value inserted, guaranteeing exactly one duplicate so the answer stays unique
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    base = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(base)
    dup = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    pos = rng.randrange(0, n + 1)
    base.insert(pos, dup)
    return [base]
